title: Choking — Child/Infant
immediate_action: If the child cannot cry, cough, or breathe — act immediately.

steps.instruction: For INFANTS: Lay face-down on your forearm, head lower than body. Support the head. Give 5 back blows between shoulder blades.
steps.tip: Use the heel of your hand. Gravity helps — keep head lower than chest.

steps.instruction: Flip infant face-up. Give 5 chest thrusts using 2 fingers on the breastbone, just below the nipple line.
steps.tip: Press about 1.5 inches deep. Quick, sharp thrusts.

steps.instruction: For CHILDREN (1-12 years): Stand or kneel behind. Give 5 back blows, then 5 abdominal thrusts (Heimlich). Same as adult but with less force.
steps.tip: Kneel to the child's level. Adjust force for size.

steps.instruction: Repeat cycles until object comes out. Check the mouth between cycles — remove visible objects only.
steps.tip: NEVER do a blind finger sweep in a child's mouth — you may push the object deeper.

steps.instruction: If the child becomes unconscious: call emergency services, begin CPR. Check mouth before each rescue breath.
steps.tip: Chest compressions during CPR may dislodge the object.

do_not: Do NOT do abdominal thrusts on infants under 1 year (use back blows + chest thrusts)
Do NOT do blind finger sweeps
Do NOT hold baby upside down by the ankles
